title: Lineage Canvas — Lineage Extract (v1.0)
description: The accepted shape for a lineage extract produced from a SAS/SQL/Snowflake script and loaded via the app's 'Upload JSON' button. The agent must output a SINGLE JSON object that validates against this schema and NOTHING else (no Markdown, no prose, no code fences). WHAT THIS CAPTURES — and ONLY this: (1) the TABLES the script reads/writes, (2) the COLUMNS that are clearly referenced, with their data types, (3) TABLE→TABLE connections, and (4) COLUMN→COLUMN connections. NUANCES: (a) Do NOT include the system (Legacy/Target) — Legacy and Target are independent flows and the user chooses the system, project, and canvas at upload time in a validation screen. (b) Do NOT include any metadata (descriptions, environment, row counts, nullability, transformation types, expressions, confidence, processes, etc.) — metadata is added by the user later; this format intentionally mirrors the simple Excel template. (c) Tables are referenced by `name` everywhere (in connections), so each table `name` must be UNIQUE within one extract. (d) UPPERCASE all identifiers (table names, namespaces, column names) — existing objects are stored uppercase, so matching avoids duplicates. (e) `namespace` is optional; if the script does not make it clear, omit it or use 'DEFAULT_UNKNOWN' (the user fixes namespaces in the validation screen). (f) Import is ADDITIVE: it creates missing tables, APPENDS new columns to existing tables, and adds connections; it never overwrites or deletes anything. So describe only what should EXIST. (g) Only include columns you can clearly tie to the script (declared in a SELECT/CREATE, or used in a connection) — do not invent columns.

schema_version.description: Schema version. MUST be exactly the string "1.0".

tables.description: Every table/dataset the script reads or writes. Optional, but include all you can identify so real tables (not bare stubs) are created. A table referenced only inside a connection but omitted here is auto-created as a minimal stub at import.

table_connections.description: Table-to-table flow. One entry per (upstream table → downstream table) relationship the script establishes. Reference tables by their `name`.

column_connections.description: Column-to-column lineage. One entry per OUTPUT column, listing the input column(s) it derives from. Reference tables by `name` and columns by `column`. This is the most valuable output — be precise and conservative.

name.description: Table/dataset name, UPPERCASE. Must be UNIQUE within this extract (it is the key used by connections). Do NOT include the namespace or system here — just the table name (e.g. 'CUSTOMERS').

namespace.description: Optional schema/library the table lives in, UPPERCASE (SAS libref, or SQL/Snowflake schema, e.g. 'SALES' or 'ANALYTICS.PUBLIC'). If the script does not make it clear, OMIT this or set it to 'DEFAULT_UNKNOWN' — the user assigns/edits namespaces in the validation screen before import.

columns.description: The columns of this table that are clearly referenced by the script (declared in SELECT/CREATE or used in a connection). UPPERCASE names. Existing tables only GAIN new columns at import; existing columns are never modified.

data_type.description: Optional data type exactly as the script expresses it (e.g. 'NUMBER', 'VARCHAR', 'CHAR', 'DATE', 'NUMERIC(18,2)'). Omit if the script does not reveal it. Do not normalize types.

from.description: Upstream table `name` (must match a table `name`, or it becomes a stub).

target.description: The OUTPUT column produced by the script.

sources.description: The INPUT column(s) the target derives from (one for a direct/renamed copy, several for combined expressions/aggregations).

table.description: Table `name` this column belongs to (UPPERCASE; must match a table `name`).
